name: Anise Hyssop
summary: Internal cross-linking supports Anise Hyssop through compounds such as limonene, anethole, estragole.
description: Internal cross-linking supports Anise Hyssop through compounds such as limonene, anethole, estragole.
contraindications: concentrated essential oil or extract because its estragole content reaches 88 to 95 percent of the essential oil and estragole is a rodent-model genotoxic carcinogen at high cumulative exposure
pregnancy or breastfeeding without clinician guidance due to estragole's carcinogenic profile and the recommendation to minimize cumulative estragole exposure during these periods
young children due to the same cumulative estragole-exposure caution and lower-estragole herbs being preferred at this life stage
known allergy to Lamiaceae family plants because Agastache foeniculum is a mint-family species

sources.title: Anise Hyssop Agastache foeniculum Increases Lifespan, Stress Resistance, and Metabolism by Affecting Free Radical Processes in Drosophila
Biologically Active Extracts from Different Medicinal Plants Tested as Potential Additives against Bee Pathogens
Estragole: a weak direct-acting food-borne genotoxin and potential carcinogen
Bridging the Chemical Profile and Biological Activities of a New Variety of Agastache foeniculum (Pursh) Kuntze Extracts and Essential Oil
